execution_summary: The replication study was built and executed inside a Docker container based on python:3.10-slim with pandas, numpy, and statsmodels. A small wrapper script (replicate_analysis.py) was created at the repository root to call the original analysis located in replication_data/. The original script was updated to use statsmodels’ MixedLM.from_formula interface for the variance-components specification. After fixing minor syntax issues, the mixed-effects model (cont ~ losses, random intercepts for participant with session variance component) ran successfully on 1,280 contribution observations. No deviations from the preregistered plan were required beyond these coding adjustments.

code_executed.logs: Mixed Linear Model converged; coefficient for losses = -0.545 (SE = 0.929, p = 0.557). Results saved to /app/data/replication_results.csv

results.hypothesis_tested: Individual contributions will be lower when the bonus distribution includes potential losses (losses = 1) than when it does not (losses = 0).
